Notes: Strong parent engagement. One family unable to attend due to work conflict - phone conference held following week.
Excellent turnout. Used student-led portfolio sharing for first time - very well received by families.
Two families with transportation barriers - offered virtual option which worked well.
Good conversations about reading progress. Two no-shows were families we've had difficulty reaching all year.
Interpreter scheduling was challenging - need to book earlier next time.
Highest turnout among 2nd grade. Ms. Okoye sends personal video invitations which families appreciate.
Three no-shows from families who also missed Back to School Night. Family liaison following up.
Extended conferences for 3 students with IEPs. Karen interpreter had to be arranged through external agency.
Some 4th grade parents mentioned they feel less connected as kids get older. Discussed ways to stay involved.
Behavior concerns were primary topic for several conferences. Co-led 2 conferences with school counselor.
Lower attendance in upper elementary is a trend. Will try student-led conferences in spring to boost engagement.
Several conferences focused on middle school transition preparation. 4 no-shows despite multiple contact attempts.
Significant drop in attendance from elementary. Piloting online scheduling tool seemed to help for some families.
Virtual option was popular with middle school parents. Several requested email updates instead of conferences.
Lowest attendance among 7th grade. Many families said student reported everything was fine. Need better pre-conference communication.
Offered evening conference slot which helped working families. Oromo interpreter was difficult to find - used phone interpretation service.
8th grade attendance continues to decline. Focus on high school transition planning helped bring some families in.
Lowest overall attendance. Tried student-led conference format for willing students - the 4 who participated had much more engaged parent conversations.
IEP families generally have higher attendance. Longer conferences due to IEP goal review. 4 conferences required interpreters.
Combined IEP progress review with conference when possible to reduce family visits. Well received.
All conferences offered in home language. Bilingual staff assisted with 3 conferences. External interpreters for 3.
